title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Command Brief: Process Model
summary: Diagnose a worker that appears hung while preserving evidence, avoiding collateral termination, and distinguishing shell jobs from system processes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux process model increment in a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.
Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Process Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2206; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Process Model contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Process Model while producing a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2206; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup. Competency probe: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources. A defensible solution applies Process Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-process-model — Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.
pgrep -a -P 2100
ps -o pid,ppid,stat,ni,cmd -p 2201
kill -TERM 2201
# Verify exit and cleanup before considering a stronger signal.
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
